title: nfs-utils bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for Oracle Linux is now available for Oracle Linux 9.
description:
[2.5.4-38.0.1]
- spec: remove multiple warnings when upgrading nfs-utils with gssproxy [Orabug: 36044562]

[2.5.4-38]
- ensure services are stopped when nfs-utils is uninstalled (RHEL-88422)

[2.5.4-37]
- gssd.man: add documentation for use-gss-proxy nfs.conf option (RHEL-85408)
- gssd: do not use krb5_cc_initialize (RHEL-85412)

[2.5.4-36]
- mountstats: verify that old and new types are the same (RHEL-88553)

[2.5.4-35]
- nfs(5): Add new rdirplus functionality, clarify (RHEL-87143)
- nfsd: allow more than 64 backlogged connections (RHEL-87752)
full_text:
[2.5.4-38.0.1]
- spec: remove multiple warnings when upgrading nfs-utils with gssproxy [Orabug: 36044562]

[2.5.4-38]
- ensure services are stopped when nfs-utils is uninstalled (RHEL-88422)

[2.5.4-37]
- gssd.man: add documentation for use-gss-proxy nfs.conf option (RHEL-85408)
- gssd: do not use krb5_cc_initialize (RHEL-85412)

[2.5.4-36]
- mountstats: verify that old and new types are the same (RHEL-88553)

[2.5.4-35]
- nfs(5): Add new rdirplus functionality, clarify (RHEL-87143)
- nfsd: allow more than 64 backlogged connections (RHEL-87752)

References:
https://linux.oracle.com/errata/ELBA-2025-20539-0.html
